items.title: VI Live: PEC op voorsprong door heerlijke pass Kostons, Telstar leidt tegen Twente
items.summary: Zondag staan er vier wedstrijden in de Eredivisie op het programma. Het duel van Feyenoord volgen we in een aparte live. In deze VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van SC Heerenveen tegen PEC Zwolle, Telstar tegen FC Twente, Sparta Rotterdam tegen NEC én het belangrijkste buitenlandse voetbal.

items.title: 'Dit erbarmelijke Feyenoord verdiende deze overwinning niet'
items.summary: Pas bij het ingaan van de extra tijd volgde er verlossing voor Feyenoord, dat toen ruim 75 minuten met een man meer had gevoetbald tegen Go Ahead Eagles. Analyticus Kees Kwakman denkt er het zijne van.

items.title: Chelsea v Liverpool, Aston Villa v Tottenham, and more: Women’s Super League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Follow WSL updates from the four 12pm GMT kick-offs ⚽ Live scoreboard | Follow us on Bluesky | Email Xaymaca What a devastating counter attack from Everton! They have an overload going forward and work it to Hannah Blundell. Blundell plays the ball into the path of Honoka Hayashi who taps into an empty net. Well this is a surprise. London City Lionesses work it well down the right and deliver the ball into the box. Nikita Parris bravely meets it and heads the ball back across goal. What a start for the away side. items.title: Macclesfield's Rooney: 'Ambition' to manage higher
items.summary: John Rooney is clear in his ambition to manage at the highest level possible but he wants to go on that journey with Macclesfield.

items.title: Juve furious at 'unacceptable' ref call in Inter loss
items.summary: Juventus officials expressed their fury and what they called an "unacceptable" and "embarrassing" decision to send off defender Pierre Kalulu in Saturday's dramatic loss to rivals Inter Milan.

items.title: Reijnders warns Arsenal: Man City 'on the hunt'
items.summary: Tijjani Reijnders has warned Arsenal that Manchester City remain firmly "on the hunt" for the Premier League title.

items.title: Vitor Pereira named new Nottingham Forest boss
items.summary: Nottingham Forest have named Vitor Pereira as their new head coach, following the sacking of Sean Dyche.

items.title: Real Madrid 'lucky to have' Trent, says Arbeloa
items.summary: Alvaro Arbeloa praised Trent Alexander-Arnold, saying Real Madrid are "lucky to have" him after the full-back starred, providing an assist, in Real Madrid's 4-1 win over Real Sociedad.

items.title: VI Live: trotsheid overheerst bij Go Ahead na strijd tegen Feyenoord
items.summary: Feyenoord krijgt zondagmiddag bezoek van Go Ahead Eagles. De Rotterdammers kwamen in rustiger vaarwater na de zege op FC Utrecht. Go Ahead wacht al zeventien duels op een zege. Volg de Eredivisie-pot in deze VI Live. Voor al het andere voetbal op de (inter)nationale velden hebben we een aparte VI Live .

items.title: Waarom Feyenoord worstelde tegen tiental Go Ahead
items.summary: Feyenoord heeft in de ultieme slotfase toch nog gewonnen van Go Ahead Eagles: 1-0. Tijd voor een tactische analyse van een wedstrijd waarin Feyenoord moeite heeft om een tegenstander met tien man uit elkaar te spelen.

items.title: Feyenoord ontsnapt door penalty na lange worsteling tegen tiental Go Ahead
items.summary: Feyenoord heeft ternauwernood pijnlijk puntenverlies in speelronde 23 voorkomen. De Rotterdammers keken heel lang tegen een 0-0 stand aan tegen een Go Ahead Eagles dat bijna de hele wedstrijd met tien man speelde, maar Casper Tengstedt redde de dag.

items.title: Pereira announced as Forest boss on 18-month deal
items.summary: Nottingham Forest appoint Vitor Pereira on an 18-month deal as the 57-year-old becomes the club's fourth manager of the campaign.

items.title: T20 World Cup: Pakistan take early India wicket after no handshake at toss LIVE!
European football: Serie A referees’ chief apologises after controversial Kalulu red card
items.summary: Juventus lost 3-2 in dramatic fashion away at Inter Spalletti and Chiellini confronted referee La Penna Serie A’s referee designator Gianluca Rocchi said match official Federico La Penna was “clearly wrong” in showing Juventus defender Pierre Kalulu a second yellow card during Saturday’s loss at Inter Milan, and apologised over the incident. Kalulu was sent off after Inter’s Alessandro Bastoni tumbled to the ground and immediately gestured towards the referee demanding a card, indicating that Kalulu had grabbed his shirt to bring him down. Television footage suggested there was no contact between the players. Juventus, down to 10 men after the sending off, lost 3-2, meaning Inter are now eight points clear at the top. This story will be updated Continue reading...

items.title: Captain Van Dijk wants 'so important' Salah to stay
items.summary: Liverpool captain hails the importance of Mohamed Salah, why the Egyptian brings more than goals and his desire for Salah to stay beyond the summer

items.title: Nottingham Forest confirm Vítor Pereira as fourth head coach of season
items.summary: Sean Dyche was sacked after 25 games in the role Pereira starts with Europa League tie at Fenerbahce Nottingham Forest have confirmed the appointment of Vítor Pereira as their fourth head coach of the season. The former Wolves manager takes over from Sean Dyche, who was sacked on Thursday, on a contract until June 2027. Pereira inherits a team one place and three points above the relegation zone. Dyche lasted 25 matches after replacing Ange Postecoglou, who was given eight games as the successor to Nuno Espírito Santo. Continue reading...

items.title: Pereira confirmed as new Forest head coach
items.summary: Vitor Pereira has been confirmed as the new Nottingham Forest head coach on an 18-month deal.

items.title: West Indies reach Super 8s with commanding Nepal win in England's group
items.summary: West Indies are through to the Super 8s of the T20 World Cup after maintaining their perfect record so far by beating Nepal by nine wickets in Mumbai.

items.title: Eden Hazard: ‘I’m more of a taxi driver than a football player now, but it’s OK’
items.summary: Former Chelsea and Real Madrid idol wants merely to be remembered as ‘a good player and a funny guy’ after a career of multiple titles – and spats with Mourinho If Italy is a boot, Lecce sits right on the heel. It is here, deep in the countryside a few kilometres outside the baroque city, that the noise of the Bernabéu and the intensity of Stamford Bridge feel like a lifetime ago. The setting is rustic, quiet and slow-paced: a stark contrast to the frenetic energy that defined Eden Hazard’s career on the pitch. It has been almost three years since he stopped playing, and the silence since his retirement at 32 has been notable. After an injury-hit spell at Real Madrid brought a premature end to a dazzling career, Hazard did not seek the spotlight. Surrounded by vineyards rather than defenders, slumped in an armchair, he seems entirely at peace, remarkably comfortable with his life after football. Continue reading...
